growth_records.cite: Madin JS et al. A synthesis of bacterial and archaeal phenotypic trait data. Sci Data 7:170 (2020); source dataset: engqvist.
growth_records.notes: Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

growth_records.notes: Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

sec.compound: polyhydroxyalkanoate (PHA; ~100% 3-hydroxybutyrate, <1 wt% 3HV; intracellular polymer, not extracellularly secreted)
sec.method: GC-MS methanolysis of lyophilized cells; PHA concentration per culture time

product_yields.PHA_per_utilized_carbon_substrate: 0.10 g/g (this study, 1 uM iron; CDM 1.89 g/L, PHA content 32.4 wt%)

growth_records.cite: Foong CP et al. Optimal iron concentrations for growth-associated polyhydroxyalkanoate biosynthesis in the marine photosynthetic purple bacterium Rhodovulum sulfidophilum under photoheterotrophic condition. PLoS ONE (2019). DOI:10.1371/journal.pone.0212654. [PMC6488045]
growth_records.snippet: The highest specific growth rate, 2.42 d-1 was achieved in the presence of 1 uM iron between Day-1 and Day-2 ... R. sulfidophilum with 1 uM iron achieved the highest productivity of 0.29 g/L/d at Day-2 ... our 520-GM supplemented with 1 uM
growth_records.notes: MEASURED specific growth rate reported as 2.42 d-1 (Day-1 to Day-2, 1 uM iron, from OD660 slope). Converted to per-hour: mu = 2.42 d-1 / 24 h/d = 0.1008 h-1. Doubling time td = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.1008 = 6.874 h (= ln2/2.42 = 0.286 d). PHA volumetric productivity 0.29 g/L/d (Day-2, 1 uM iron) is a whole-culture volumetric rate, NOT a biomass-specific mmol/gDW/h rate; PHA is intracellular so listed under secretion_rates only as the paper's product-formation metric. PHA yield 0.10 g PHA per g utilized carbon substrate (this study's own value: CDM 1.89 g/L, PHA content 32.4 wt%). No substrate uptake rate (malate/pyruvate consumption) is reported numerically, so qS could NOT be computed (Yxs biomass/substrate not given). Max CDM ~2.5-2.8 g/L at stationary phase. Carbon sources: sodium L-malate + sodium pyruvate. Comparison value 0.05 g/g (sodium acetate; CDM 0.42 g/L; PHA 56.1 wt%) is CITED from Chowdhury et al. ref [23], NOT measured here.

growth_records.cite: Foong CP et al. Optimal iron concentrations for growth-associated polyhydroxyalkanoate biosynthesis in the marine photosynthetic purple bacterium Rhodovulum sulfidophilum under photoheterotrophic condition. PLoS ONE (2019). DOI:10.1371/journal.pone.0212654. [PMC6488045]
growth_records.snippet: Both CDM and OD660 increased drastically from 2.14 g/L to 3.89 g/L and from 4.26 to 8.71, respectively, within 24 h cultivation period. However, no changes were observed in PHA content (21 to 22 wt%).
growth_records.notes: Growth rate CALCULATED from reported CDM increase during the iron-free second-stage: CDM 2.14 -> 3.89 g/L over 24 h (1 day). mu = ln(3.89/2.14)/1 d = ln(1.8178)/1 = 0.5976 d-1 = 0.5976/24 = 0.0249 h-1; td = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.0249 = 27.84 h. (An alternative OD660-based estimate: ln(8.71/4.26)/1 d = 0.7152 d-1 = 0.0298 h-1, but OD660 overreads due to PHA-granule light scattering per the authors, so the CDM-based value is preferred.) Distinct condition from record 1 (iron-free, resuspended stationary-phase cells). No substrate uptake or secretion RATE (mmol/gDW/h) reported; PHA is intracellular and its content was flat (21->22 wt%). Culture entered stationary phase after 24 h due to nutrient depletion.
